data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/1 went from oper=up to oper=lower-layer-down while admin remains up; R3 Serial0/1/1 went from oper=up to oper=down and is administratively down. Impact: loss of serial link between R2 and R3. Desired outcome: restore the serial link (oper up) by reconciling administrative state.

plan.problem: Serial link between R2 and R3 is down: R2 Serial0/1/1 oper=lower-layer-down (admin up); R3 Serial0/1/1 oper=down (admin down). Likely cause: R3 interface is administratively shut.
plan.fix_summary: Remove administrative shutdown on R3 Serial0/1/1 (set admin up) to restore the link. If link does not return to oper up, perform further diagnostics (log review, provider circuit checks).

messages.content:
What I observed
- R2 Serial0/1/1 showed oper went to lower-layer-down while admin remained up; R3 Serial0/1/1 was oper down and administratively down. Result: loss of the R2–R3 serial link.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Likely administrative shutdown on R3 (R3 admin down caused the mismatch and link loss).

If changes were made
- Attempted to set R3 Serial0/1/1 admin up. The device reported the interface was already enabled (no config change required).

Verification
- Passed. Current state: R2 Serial0/1/1 = admin up, oper up; R3 Serial0/1/1 = admin up, oper up. OSPF adjacency on S0/1/1 is FULL on both routers and R3 running-config shows no "shutdown" on the interface.
- Remaining: no outstanding connectivity issues. For a complete incident audit, please provide the exact alert timestamps, interface event/syslog records, and any config-change/audit entries around the event window (these items are still missing).
